name: lexoffice
description: Manage invoices, vouchers, contacts, and receipt uploads in Lexware Office (formerly lexoffice) bookkeeping

envVars.name: LEXOFFICE_API_KEY
envVars.description: Lexware Office (lexoffice) Public API key, created in the Lexware Office app under Add-ons > Public API

tools.name: List Vouchers
tools.description: List and filter bookkeeping vouchers (sales and purchase invoices, credit notes) via the voucherlist, e.g. to find open or overdue invoices

voucherType.description: Required. Comma-separated voucher types or 'any'. Types include salesinvoice, salescreditnote, purchaseinvoice, purchasecreditnote, invoice, downpaymentinvoice, creditnote, orderconfirmation, quotation, deliverynote.

voucherStatus.description: Required. Comma-separated statuses or 'any'. Statuses include draft, open, paid, paidoff, voided, transferred, sepadebit, overdue, accepted, rejected, and unchecked (bookkeeping vouchers created via file upload that still lack mandatory information).

contactId.description: Only vouchers for this contact UUID

tools.name: Get Invoice
tools.description: Retrieve a single invoice with line items, totals, tax conditions, and payment status

invoiceId.description: Invoice UUID, e.g. from the voucherlist

tools.name: Create Invoice
tools.description: Create a sales invoice. Created as an editable draft by default; set finalize=true to immediately open it

finalize.description: If true the invoice is finalized with status open instead of draft

voucherDate.description: Invoice date in RFC 3339 format, e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00:00.000+02:00

address.description: Recipient address: either { contactId } of an existing contact or a manual address with name, street, city, zip, countryCode

lineItems.description: Line items, each with type (custom or text), name, quantity, unitName, and unitPrice { currency, netAmount, taxRatePercentage }

shippingConditions.description: Optional shipping conditions with shippingDate and shippingType

title.description: Optional document title, defaults to Rechnung

introduction.description: Optional introduction text above the line items

remark.description: Optional closing remark below the line items

tools.name: List Contacts
tools.description: List and filter customers and vendors, e.g. to find the contactId for an invoice

email.description: Filter by email address (substring match, min 3 characters)

name.description: Filter by company or person name (substring match, min 3 characters)

number.description: Filter by customer or vendor number

customer.description: If true only contacts with a customer role; if false only contacts without one

vendor.description: If true only contacts with a vendor role; if false only contacts without one

tools.name: Create Contact
tools.description: Create a customer or vendor contact for use on invoices and vouchers

version.description: Optimistic locking version, must be 0 when creating

company.description: Company details with name and optional taxNumber, vatRegistrationId, contactPersons. Provide exactly one of company or person.

person.description: Person details with firstName and lastName. Provide exactly one of company or person.

emailAddresses.description: Optional email addresses grouped by type, e.g. { "business": ["a@b.de"] }

phoneNumbers.description: Optional phone numbers grouped by type, e.g. { "business": ["+49..."] }

tools.name: Upload Voucher File
tools.description: Upload a receipt image or invoice file (pdf, jpg, png, xml; max 5 MB) for bookkeeping; returns the file id and the id of the voucher created for it

file.description: Base64-encoded file content, sent decoded as the binary multipart 'file' part. Allowed formats: pdf, jpg, png, xml; max 5 MB.

file_name.description: Filename for the uploaded file, e.g. receipt.pdf; the extension must match one of the allowed formats

tools.name: Download File
tools.description: Download an uploaded bookkeeping voucher file (receipt/invoice image) as binary data by its file id; Content-Type and Content-Disposition response headers carry the MIME type and suggested filename. For sales voucher documents prefer the document render flow.

fileId.description: File id (UUID), e.g. from Upload Voucher File or a voucher's files list

tools.name: Get Invoice Document
tools.description: Trigger PDF rendering for an invoice and return its documentFileId, which can then be downloaded via Download File. Required for invoices created through the API with status open; draft invoices have no document file and are rejected with 406.

tools.name: Get Payments
tools.description: Get the payment status of an invoice or voucher: open amount, paid date, and payment items — the reliable way to check who actually paid

voucherId.description: Id (UUID) of the invoice, credit note, or voucher to read payment information for

prompts.title: Open invoices
prompts.prompt: List my open and overdue invoices in Lexware Office and summarize the outstanding amounts.

prompts.title: Draft an invoice
prompts.prompt: Create a draft invoice in Lexware Office for a customer; ask me for the line items first.

prompts.title: File a receipt
prompts.prompt: Upload this receipt to Lexware Office as a bookkeeping voucher.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Lexware Office setup

steps.title: Create a Lexware Office account
steps.description: Sign up at https://www.lexware.de (Lexware Office, formerly lexoffice). A regular trial account is sufficient for testing the Public API; there is no separate sandbox environment.

steps.title: Generate an API key
steps.description: Log in to the Lexware Office app and open https://app.lexware.de/addons/public-api to create your Public API key. Only account administrators can create keys.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Set the key as LEXOFFICE_API_KEY. It is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header on every request.

notes: Since the rebranding from lexoffice to Lexware, the canonical API gateway is https://api.lexware.io (changed 26 May 2025). The legacy gateway api.lexoffice.io was only kept available until December 2025, so all tools use the new domain.
The API is rate limited to 2 requests per second per client; HTTP 429 is returned when exceeded.
POST /v1/files is a multipart/form-data upload: the binary file goes in the 'file' part and the upload type ('voucher') in the 'type' part.
Invoices created via the API are drafts by default; pass finalize=true to create them with status open. Finalized invoices can no longer be edited freely.
voucherType and voucherStatus are mandatory on the voucherlist endpoint; use 'any' to avoid filtering.
